steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is 'behavioral economists such as Gino,' a plural noun phrase. The blank verb must agree with a plural subject in present tense.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'contend': plural present tense, correctly agrees with the plural subject 'behavioral economists.' ✓
  • Choice B — 'is contending': singular verb, violates subject-verb agreement with the plural subject. ✗
  • Choice C — 'has contended': singular verb in present perfect, disagrees with the plural subject. ✗
  • Choice D — 'contends': singular present tense, violates agreement with the plural subject. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
